title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction
description: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. Learners cumulatively produce an accessible interactive service-status interface through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.
